untranslatable_reason: This signal requires multi-leg options arbitrage logic (box spread fair value calculation) which cannot be expressed using single-instrument technical indicators. The rule depends on: (1) deterministic box value calculations from strike/rate/expiration relationships, (2) relative pricing of two separate vertical spreads, (3) implied fair value inference from bid-ask spreads on multiple legs. None of these can be translated using the allowed indicators (price, volume, RSI, ATR, moving averages, etc.), which operate on single instrument time-series data. This is a structural arbitrage rule requiring order book and multi-leg options pricing data.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_107 describes a box spread arbitrage strategy where fair value is inferred from the relationship: Bull Call Spread + Bear Put Spread = Box Value. The signal requires comparing observed market prices of two separate spreads against a deterministically calculated box value. Thresholds for divergence_threshold_pct and min_time_to_expiration_days are marked as [THRESHOLD_NEEDED] and cannot be inferred from source text. This requires options-specific data structures and pricing models unavailable in a price-action backtest engine.
